closest to :

= 2.10 × 106 J kg–1 and Latent heat of Fusion
of water = 3.36 × 105 J kg–1)

from its equilibrium position and released, the
number of oscillation it performs before its

mass of the strip is 50 grams, its resistance 10W
and air drag negligible, N will be close to :

is 5 cm, the Reynolds number for the flow is

coefficient of viscosity of water = 1mPas)

question_text:

a charge of 5.0 μC. It is at rest in a uniform
horizontal electric field of intensity 2000 V/m.
At equilibrium, the angle that the pendulum
makes with the vertical is : (take g = 10 m/s2)

subject: Physics

a magnetic field B$${\mathop i\limits^ \wedge  }$$ . The torque on the coil due

subject: Physics
micro_topic: Stress Strain Curve Thermal Stress And Elastic Pe
question_text:
A boy's catapult is made of rubber cord which
is 42 cm long, with 6 mm diameter of
cross-section and of negligible mass. The boy
keeps a stone weighing 0.02kg on it and
stretches the cord by 20 cm by applying a
constant force. When released, the stone flies
off with a velocity of 20 ms–1. Neglect the
change in the area of cross-section of the cord
while stretched. The Young's modulus of
rubber is closest to:

subject: Physics
question_text:
Voltage rating of a parallel plate capacitor is
500V. Its dielectric can withstand a maximum
electric field of 106 V/m. The plate area is
10–4 m2. What is the dielectric constant is the
capacitance is 15 pF?
(given $$\varepsilon $$0 = 8.86 × 10–12 C2/Nm2)

subject: Physics
question_text:
Two identical beakers A and B contain equal
volumes of two different liquids at 60°C each
and left to cool down. Liquid in A has density
of 8 × 102 kg/m3 and specific heat of
2000 J kg–1 K–1 while liquid in B has density
of 103 kg m–3 and specific heat of
4000 J kg–1 K–1. Which of the following best
describes their temperature versus time graph
schematically? (assume the emissivity of both
the beakers to be the same)

subject: Physics
question_text:

mA = m, mB = 2m, mC = 3m and mD = 4m are
at the corners of a square. They have
accelerations of equal magnitude with
directions as shown. The acceleration of the
centre of mass of the particles is :
